nurse_assessment: Infant is in good condition and due for measles subcutaneous injection.
recommendation: Administer Measles vaccine subcutaneously; Observe child for 15 minutes post-injection; Record in card

nurse_assessment: Patient returns for monthly refill of oral contraceptive pills.
recommendation: Dispense 3-month supply of combined oral contraceptive; Remind patient to take daily at the same time

nurse_assessment: Patient presents for scheduled DMPA injectable contraceptive.
recommendation: Administer DMPA injectable; Counsel on breastfeeding compatibility; Schedule next injection in 3 months

nurse_assessment: Patient reports painful urination and lower back discomfort.
recommendation: Advise increase in fluid intake especially water; Request urinalysis; Refer to physician for antibiotic prescription; Advise proper hygiene

nurse_assessment: Child is in good condition and due for MMR subcutaneous injection.
recommendation: Administer MMR vaccine subcutaneously; Observe for 15 minutes post-injection; Record in immunization card; Schedule booster

nurse_assessment: Patient presents with arthritic pain affecting daily activities.
recommendation: Advise joint protection and light exercise; Apply warm compress; Give analgesic; Refer to physician for long-term management plan

nurse_assessment: Patient requests guidance on starting oral contraceptive pills.
recommendation: Counsel on oral contraceptive options; Discuss benefits and possible side effects; Dispense initial supply and set follow-up schedule

nurse_assessment: Child brought for measles immunization with complete vaccine records.
recommendation: Administer Measles vaccine subcutaneously; Observe child for 15 minutes post-injection; Record in card

nurse_assessment: Patient presents for DMPA injection and reports no side effects.
recommendation: Administer DMPA injectable; Counsel on breastfeeding compatibility; Schedule next injection in 3 months

nurse_assessment: Patient has fever with retro-orbital pain and joint aches noted.
recommendation: Urgent referral to physician; Monitor vital signs every 30 minutes; Check for bleeding signs; Encourage oral rehydration if conscious

nurse_assessment: Patient presents with rash accompanied by itching and mild swelling.
recommendation: Assess type and extent of skin rash; Give antihistamine for itching; Advise to avoid scratching; Refer to physician if spreading

nurse_assessment: Patient reports severe body pain with fever for more than 3 days.
recommendation: Urgent referral to physician; Monitor vital signs every 30 minutes; Check for bleeding signs; Encourage oral rehydration if conscious

nurse_assessment: Patient reports not taking antihypertensive medication for several days.
recommendation: Urgent referral to physician; Monitor BP every 15 minutes; Advise smoking cessation; Counsel on low-sodium diet

nurse_assessment: Patient presents with chest discomfort and elevated heart rate.
recommendation: Urgent physician referral; Monitor vital signs closely; Do not leave patient unattended

nurse_assessment: Patient comes for IUD checkup and reports occasional spotting.
recommendation: Check IUD strings for proper placement; Assess for any side effects or complications; Schedule annual follow-up

nurse_assessment: Patient presents with vomiting and abdominal cramping.
recommendation: Assess for dehydration; Administer ORS; Advise light diet; Refer to physician if vomiting persists or blood is present

nurse_assessment: Patient reports stomach pain after eating with bloating sensation.
recommendation: Assess location and nature of pain; Advise light diet and rest; Refer to physician if pain is severe or persistent

nurse_assessment: Patient requests family planning consultation after second delivery.
recommendation: Counsel on smoking cessation; Discuss suitable family planning methods based on health history; Schedule follow-up in 1 month

nurse_assessment: Patient presents for family planning consultation.
recommendation: Counsel on smoking cessation; Discuss suitable non-hormonal family planning methods; Schedule follow-up in 1 month

nurse_assessment: Patient presents with arthritic pain affecting daily activities.
recommendation: Advise joint protection and light exercise; Apply warm compress; Give analgesic; Refer to physician for long-term management plan

nurse_assessment: Patient reports fever onset 1 day ago with chills and body malaise.
recommendation: Administer antipyretic; Advise tepid sponge bath; Refer to physician if fever exceeds 39C

nurse_assessment: Patient presents with headache and very high blood pressure reading.
recommendation: Urgent referral to physician; Monitor BP every 15 minutes; Advise smoking cessation; Counsel on low-sodium diet

nurse_assessment: Patient presents for tetanus vaccine with no contraindications noted.
recommendation: Administer tetanus toxoid IM on deltoid; Record in immunization card; Advise on possible soreness at injection site

nurse_assessment: Patient requests guidance on family planning suitable for smokers.
recommendation: Counsel on smoking cessation; Discuss suitable family planning methods based on health history; Schedule follow-up in 1 month

nurse_assessment: Patient appears anxious with persistently high blood pressure noted.
recommendation: Urgent referral to physician; Monitor BP every 15 minutes; Advise smoking cessation; Counsel on low-sodium diet
